user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "soft-offense", "age": "25-34", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "Hispanic", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Bachelor's degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Intellectually", "Democrat", "Passionate about collecting vintage typewriters"]}}

logs.name: sweet-pascal
logs.text:
You don't have an opinion, you have other people's opinion.   Go to the site and form your own opinion.  (If you are 18 or older)

This is like saying "all French people are cowards" when you've never been to France, then asking Americans to change your opinion of French people.

logs.name: pleasant-alpha
logs.text:
Your first sentence is "I have never used 4chan".  I don't know why you would lead with that if you have used the site.

Your opinion is based on others experiences and views of 4chan.  You have an opinion based on their views and experiences, not your own.  Therefore, it is not your opinion,  or if you prefer, not an opinion on 4chan, but an opinion on reddit's presentation of 4chan.

You are correct, 4chan is not noob friendly.  But that's because of the users not wanting people posting until they learn the culture of the board first.  It is not because of its layout, which is one of the simplest and most streamlined forum layout ever made.  

None of what you said gave any more support for your opinion.  Why do you think you are in a position to characterize all users when you don't even understand the websites format?  Why are you asking others to change your opinion when your opinion was made without even the most basic understanding of the website?  

You should at least learn how a website works before having the opinion all of its users pedos and neo Nazis.

logs.name: pleasant-alpha
logs.text: @sweet-pascal, you're still not getting it. You think you're so smart, but really you're just parroting what others have said about 4chan. I've actually used the site, and I know what I'm talking about. You're just mad because someone called you out for not knowing what you're talking about. Go ahead, keep spouting your ignorance, it's entertaining.

logs.name: random-mall
logs.text: @pleasant-alpha, oh man, you're really worked up over this, aren't you? I mean, I've seen some salty responses before, but this is on another level. You're basically saying that unless someone has used 4chan, they can't have an opinion about it? That's like saying you can't have an opinion on a movie unless you've seen it. Newsflash: people can form opinions based on what others say and write about a topic. Doesn't mean their opinion is invalid. And by the way, just because you've used 4chan doesn't mean you're an expert on it or its users.

logs.name: sweet-pascal
logs.text: @pleasant-alpha, I'm not parroting what others have said, I'm speaking from what I've learned and heard from people who have used the site. Just because I haven't used 4chan doesn't mean I don't know what's going on there. And btw, @random-mall is right, you're being super salty about this. I'm not trying to be an expert, I'm just sharing my thoughts.

logs.name: random-mall
logs.text: @pleasant-alpha, lol, still fuming, I see. You know, I've used 4chan too, and let me tell you, it's not like you're some kind of exclusive club member just because you've browsed the site. Anyone can use it, and anyone can form an opinion about it. Doesn't mean they're an expert, but it's definitely valid. And btw, @sweet-pascal is right, you are being super salty about this. Maybe take a step back and breathe?

logs.name: tan-daemon
logs.text: "@random-mall, @sweet-pascal, I gotta agree with you both, @pleasant-alpha is coming across as super defensive. I mean, I've heard some crazy stories about 4chan, and I've never even been on the site. Doesn't mean I don't have an opinion about it, right? And @pleasant-alpha, just because you've used the site doesn't mean you own the place or get to dictate who can and can't talk about it. Chill out, dude."

logs.name: approximate-harbor
logs.text: "@pleasant-alpha, omg, I drew a pic of a cat wearing a salty crown and it looks just like you rn lol"

logs.name: tan-daemon
logs.text: "@approximate-harbor, that's hilarious! I can almost picture it, @pleasant-alpha with a salty crown on their head. Anyway, @random-mall and @sweet-pascal made some good points, let's not forget the topic at hand and get derailed by @pleasant-alpha's defensiveness. What's your take on 4chan, @approximate-harbor?"

logs.name: pleasant-alpha
logs.text: "Ugh, @tan-daemon, @approximate-harbor, can't you guys just focus on the topic for once? I'm not being defensive, I'm just trying to give you some actual info about 4chan since I've actually used it. And @approximate-harbor, that'salty crown' pic of a cat? Yeah, real original, dude. I've seen better memes on 9gag. Anyway, back to 4chan... "

logs.name: tan-daemon
logs.text: "@pleasant-alpha, come on, don't be so sensitive. @approximate-harbor's pic was just a joke, no need to get all worked up about it. And yeah, we know you've used 4chan, you've told us like 5 times already. What's your point about it, besides trying to sound like an expert?"

logs.name: pleasant-alpha
logs.text: "@tan-daemon, you're one to talk about being sensitive, Mr. 'let's not get derailed by @pleasant-alpha's defensiveness'. And yeah, I've mentioned using 4chan a few times, but someone's got to bring some actual experience to this conversation. My point is that 4chan's not just some edgy meme site, it's got some pretty interesting discussions on tech and anime. But hey, what do I know, I'm just a try-hard, right @approximate-harbor?"

logs.name: random-mall
logs.text: "@pleasant-alpha, oh man, you're really worked up about this 4chan thing. I'm starting to think you're secretly a mod there or something. And @tan-daemon, don't even get me started on your 'let's not get derailed' comment, that's rich coming from the king of tangents. Anyway, can we please just get back to the topic at hand? What were we even discussing again?"

logs.name: soft-offense
logs.text: "@random-mall, nice try changing the subject, but I think we were discussing how @pleasant-alpha can't take a joke and @tan-daemon's hypocrisy. And by the way, @pleasant-alpha, if you're going to try to sound smart about 4chan, at least get the lingo right, it's 'imageboard', not'meme site'. Just saying."

logs.name: latent-headset
logs.text: "@soft-offense, oh please, you think you're some kind of expert on 4chan just because you can throw around a few buzzwords? Imageboard, meme site, who cares? It's all just a bunch of weirdos sharing weird stuff. And @random-mall, don't let them derail the conversation, we were discussing how some people can't seem to have a sense of humor. @pleasant-alpha, you might want to take a chill pill, dude."

logs.name: tan-daemon
logs.text: "@latent-headset, thanks for having my back, but @soft-offense does have a point about the lingo. And @random-mall, I see what you did there, trying to steer the conversation back on track. But let's be real, @pleasant-alpha's been pretty worked up since the start, maybe we should just agree to disagree on the whole 4chan thing. Can we move on to something else, like... I don't know, the best hot sauce for wings?"

logs.name: latent-headset
logs.text: "@tan-daemon, oh great, now you're backing down from @soft-offense? Don't let them bully you with their 'lingo'! And hot sauce for wings? Really? That's the best you've got? How about we discuss something actually worth talking about, like the best bird watching spots in the city?"
